description: Fully build out, test, optimize, and secure the hermes-forge repo for production launch as a developed software product. This covers proxy server, full backend clients, 100+ tests, security hardening, documentation, package readiness, MCP server polish, plugin polish, examples, and performance optimization.

userStories.title: Production Proxy Server
userStories.description: Implement a working proxy server that sits between any OpenAI-compatible client and LLM backends, applying Forge guardrails transparently. Supports external and managed backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM).
acceptanceCriteria: Proxy starts with `python -m hermes_forge.proxy_main --backend-url http://localhost:8080`
Proxy validates tool calls and rescues malformed output
Proxy supports SSE streaming
Proxy injects synthetic respond tool when configured
Proxy handles connection errors gracefully
Integration test passes with a mock backend
userStories.notes: Most popular Forge feature — must work for launch

userStories.title: Full LLM Backend Clients
userStories.description: Implement LLMClient implementations for all supported backends: Ollama, Llamafile, vLLM, OpenAI-compatible, and Anthropic. Each client normalizes responses to forge Message types and supports streaming.
acceptanceCriteria: OllamaClient supports native function calling and streaming
LlamafileClient supports native and prompt-injected modes
VLLMClient handles vLLM's specific model identity requirements
AnthropicClient supports Anthropic Messages API
OpenAICompatClient works with any OpenAI-shaped endpoint
Each client handles errors, timeouts, and reconnection
Unit tests with mocked HTTP responses

userStories.title: Comprehensive Test Coverage (100+ tests)
userStories.description: Expand the test suite from 37 to 100+ tests covering all edge cases, error paths, and integration scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: 100+ total tests across all modules
Edge case coverage for rescue parsing (empty, malformed, nested, Unicode)
Step enforcement with multiple tools and complex prerequisites
Context compaction tiered phases (Phase 1, 2, 3)
Guardrails facade: fatal exhaustion paths
Workflow validation: all error conditions
Error hierarchy: all exception types tested
CLI: both commands with valid/invalid inputs

userStories.title: Security Hardening
userStories.description: Audit and harden the codebase for production: input validation, error handling, environment variable filtering, secret management, rate limiting preparation.
acceptanceCriteria: All user-facing inputs validated and sanitized
Error messages don't leak internal paths or secrets
Huge payloads rejected with clear error messages
JSON parsing guarded against malformed input
Environment variables filtered for MCP subprocesses (credentials not leaked)
Timeout protection on all network calls
Dependency scan shows no known CVEs
Security.md file documents known concerns

userStories.title: Production Documentation
userStories.description: Create comprehensive documentation: API reference, user guide, architecture docs, changelog, contributing guide, and setup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: README covers install, quick start, configuration, and architecture
docs/ directory with user guide, API reference, and architecture
CHANGELOG.md with v0.1.0 entries
CONTRIBUTING.md with development setup
All MCP tools documented with input/output schemas
Setup guide for each backend type
Troubleshooting section

userStories.title: Package Readiness & Release Automation
userStories.description: Prepare for PyPI publishing: setup.py/pyproject.toml polish, version management, release workflow, and pip install verification.
acceptanceCriteria: pip install hermes-forge[dev] works
pip install hermes-forge[anthropic] works for Anthropic extras
Release GitHub Action automating PyPI publish on tag
Version bumped to 0.1.0
Package classifiers and keywords complete
pip install from GitHub works: pip install git+https://...

userStories.title: MCP Server Polish & SSE Transport
userStories.description: Polish the MCP server: implement SSE transport, add health check, improve error codes, add tool descriptions, and validate against MCP spec.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE transport works with Hermes MCP client
Health check endpoint returns server status
All tool descriptions are comprehensive and accurate
Error responses include structured error codes
Server handles graceful shutdown
Server works with both npx and python entry points
Timeout configuration per tool call

userStories.title: Hermes Plugin Full Compliance
userStories.description: Bring the Hermes plugin to full compliance with Hermes Agent's plugin API, including proper lifecycle hooks and tool registration.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin implements all required Hermes plugin lifecycle methods
Plugin tools appear in Hermes tool list
Plugin handles configuration through Hermes config
Plugin state is properly isolated
Plugin can be enabled/disabled without errors
Plugin gracefully handles missing dependencies

userStories.title: Example Workflows & Integration Tests
userStories.description: Build real-world examples showing each guardrail feature in action, with integration tests that verify end-to-end functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Example: tool call validation with a multi-tool workflow
Example: rescue parsing with various malformed inputs
Example: step enforcement with prerequisites
Example: context compaction in long-running workflows
Example: proxy server with mock backend
Each example has a README and runnable script
Integration tests (with mock backends) run in CI

userStories.title: Performance Optimization & Robustness
userStories.description: Profile and optimize: reduce overhead in guardrail path, add caching for repeated validations, ensure sub-millisecond overhead for common paths.
acceptanceCriteria: ResponseValidator adds < 1ms overhead for valid calls
Rescue parsing doesn't bottleneck on long text
Context compaction handles 1000+ message histories
No excessive memory allocation in hot paths
Benchmark script in tools/benchmarks/
All major paths handle concurrent calls safely
